title: Willis Towers Watson: More Macro Risk, But Also More Room For Improvement
text: Willis Towers Watson had a better than expected fourth quarter, with the company returning to inline organic growth performance with its peer group and beating on margins. Insurance brokerage should remain healthy in 2023, particularly on the consulting side, and while a recession would threaten the HWC business, I don't expect significant layoffs.

title: Willis Towers Watson Public Limited Company (WTW) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Willis Towers Watson Public Limited Company (NASDAQ:WTW ) Q4 2022 Earnings Conference Call February 9, 2023 9:00 AM ET Company Participants Carl Hess – Chief Executive Officer Andrew Krasner – Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Elyse Greenspan – Wells Fargo Gregory Peters – Raymond James David Motemaden – Evercore Michael Zaremski – BMO Paul Newsome – Piper Sandler Andrew Kligerman – Credit Suisse Rob Cox – Goldman Sachs Joshua Shanker – Bank of America Brian Meredith – UBS Mark Hughes – Truist Derek Han – KBW Mark Marcon – Baird Operator Good morning. Welcome to the WTW Fourth Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call.

title: Willis Towers (WTW) Q4 Earnings Top Estimates, Increase Y/Y
text: Willis Towers (WTW) Q4 results reflect the solid performance of the Health, Wealth & Career segment and lower expenses, which were offset by a soft performance in the Risk & Broking segment.

title: Willis Towers Watson (WTW) Q4 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Willis Towers Watson (WTW) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 0.64% and 1.44%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Here's What to Expect from Willis Towers' (WTW) Q4 Earnings
text: Willis Towers' (WTW) fourth-quarter results are likely to reflect the impact of higher levels of regulatory work in Great Britain and increased project activity, growth in Medicare Advantage revenues, increases in advisory work and product sale.
